steps.title: Understand what the discoveries do
steps.content:
The passage says archaeologists found a coin dated 337–341 CE and era-appropriate building materials—both of which align with the fourth-century theory. The blank must describe what those discoveries do for the theory: they supply or furnish supporting evidence, not reject or control it.
  • "dismiss": dismiss means reject or disregard—the opposite of what the discoveries do. ✗
  • "provide": provide means make available or supply—the discoveries supply evidence for the dating claim. ✓
  • "regulate": regulate means govern or control; nothing in the passage suggests the discoveries govern how evidence is managed. ✗
  • "refuse": refuse means be unwilling to accept—another opposite of supplying support. ✗

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: Only “provide” correctly describes discoveries that furnish supporting evidence for a theory. That matches the correct answer — B is correct.
